section_name: Vehicles Carrying Explosive Substances or Flammable Liquids
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 545. Operation & Movement of Vehicles
##### § 545.254 — Vehicles Carrying Explosive Substances or Flammable Liquids

(a) Before crossing a railroad grade crossing, an operator of a vehicle that has an explosive substance or flammable liquid as the vehicle’s principal cargo and that is moving at a speed of more than 20 miles per hour:

    (1) shall reduce the speed of the vehicle to 20 miles per hour or less before coming within 200 feet of the nearest rail of the railroad;

    (2) shall listen and look in both directions along the track for an approaching train or other on-track equipment and for signals indicating the approach of a train or other on-track equipment; and

    (3) may not proceed until the operator determines that the course is clear.

(b) The operator of a vehicle that has an explosive substance or flammable liquid as the vehicle’s principal cargo, before crossing a railroad grade crossing on a highway in a municipality:

    (1) shall stop the vehicle not closer than 15 feet or farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail of the railroad;

    (2) while stopped, shall listen and look in both directions along the track for an approaching train or other on-track equipment and for signals indicating the approach of a train or other on-track equipment; and

    (3) may not proceed until the operator determines that the course is clear.

(c) Subsections (a) and (b) do not apply:

    (1) if a police officer, crossing flagger, or traffic-control signal directs traffic to proceed;

    (2) where a railroad flashing signal is installed and does not indicate an approaching train or other on-track equipment;

    (3) to an abandoned or exempted grade crossing that is clearly marked by or with the consent of the state, if the markings can be read from the operator’s location;

    (4) at a streetcar crossing in a business or residential district of a municipality; or

    (5) to a railroad track used exclusively for industrial switching purposes in a business district.

(d) This section does not exempt the operator from compliance with Section 545.251 (Obedience to Signal Indicating Approach of Train or Other On-track Equipment) or 545.252 (All Vehicles to Stop at Certain Railroad Grade Crossings) .

(e) An offense under this section is punishable by a fine of not less than $50 or more than $200.

*Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 128 (H.B.1759), Sec. 6, eff. September 1, 2021.*

subsections.text: Before crossing a railroad grade crossing, an operator of a vehicle that has an explosive substance or flammable liquid as the vehicle’s principal cargo and that is moving at a speed of more than 20 miles per hour:
shall reduce the speed of the vehicle to 20 miles per hour or less before coming within 200 feet of the nearest rail of the railroad;
shall listen and look in both directions along the track for an approaching train or other on-track equipment and for signals indicating the approach of a train or other on-track equipment; and
may not proceed until the operator determines that the course is clear.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The operator of a vehicle that has an explosive substance or flammable liquid as the vehicle’s principal cargo, before crossing a railroad grade crossing on a highway in a municipality:
shall stop the vehicle not closer than 15 feet or farther than 50 feet from the nearest rail of the railroad;

while stopped, shall listen and look in both directions along the track for an approaching train or other on-track equipment and for signals indicating the approach of a train or other on-track equipment; and
may not proceed until the operator determines that the course is clear.
if a police officer, crossing flagger, or traffic-control signal directs traffic to proceed;
where a railroad flashing signal is installed and does not indicate an approaching train or other on-track equipment;
to an abandoned or exempted grade crossing that is clearly marked by or with the consent of the state, if the markings can be read from the operator’s location;
at a streetcar crossing in a business or residential district of a municipality; or
to a railroad track used exclusively for industrial switching purposes in a business district.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: This section does not exempt the operator from compliance with Section 545.251 (Obedience to Signal Indicating Approach of Train or Other On-track Equipment) or 545.252 (All Vehicles to Stop at Certain Railroad Grade Crossings) .

subsections.label: e
